commits.subject: test(0.8.0): Slice 25 fix-1 — add single-source governed-surface allowlist contract (RED scaffold for Py/TS parity)
fix(0.8.0): Slice 25 fix-1 — governed-surface tests enforce real introspection + shared-contract parity (codex 2x[P1])

findings_addressed.title: Live command surface was hard-coded, so the allowlist membership check passed vacuously
findings_addressed.fix: Fix A — both bindings now build the live command surface by genuine introspection. Python: _live_python_command_surface() iterates dir(Engine), strips dunder/private names and the non-command members (_INSTRUMENTATION plus open_report/path/config), and introspects admin.__all__. TypeScript: liveTsCommandSurface() enumerates Engine statics + prototype/own instance function names (the no-recovery-surface.test.ts style) minus the non-command members. No hard-coded verb list is the source of `live` in either binding; a new public command (e.g. Engine.delete) would now surface as an extra and fail test_public_surface_is_allowlist.

findings_addressed.title: Parity test compared against a same-file duplicate literal, so cross-binding drift was undetectable
findings_addressed.fix: Fix B — the allowlist now has a single declaration at src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json. Python loads it via _load_governed_surface_contract() (Path(__file__).parents[2]/conformance/...); TypeScript loads the same file via loadGovernedSurfaceContract() (fileURLToPath(import.meta.url), resolved from dist/tests/ up to src/conformance). Both same-file duplicate literals are removed. The parity test pins that each suite consumes the shared contract; divergence is structurally impossible because there is only one copy.

fix_a_python.red_perturbation: Monkeypatched Engine.delete = lambda self: None then called test_public_surface_is_allowlist via the imported module.
fix_a_python.red_result: FAILED as expected: AssertionError 'live command(s) outside the governed allowlist: [\'delete\']'. Perturbation removed (del Engine.delete).
fix_a_python.green_after: test_public_surface_is_allowlist passes with the perturbation removed (live surface = five core, subset of 9-member allowlist).

fix_a_ts.red_perturbation: Set Engine.prototype.delete = function(){} in a throwaway scratch script reusing liveTsCommandSurface logic against compiled dist.
fix_a_ts.red_result: RED-OK: live command(s) outside allowlist: ['delete']. Perturbation removed (delete Engine.prototype.delete); scratch script deleted.
fix_a_ts.green_after: surface.test.ts 'public surface is the governed allowlist' passes; introspected live surface is a subset of the shared allowlist.

fix_b_python.red_perturbation: Removed 'write' from src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json (simulates Py-vs-contract / cross-binding drift), then ran pytest on test_public_surface_is_allowlist and test_surface_parity_py_matches_ts.
fix_b_python.red_result: 2 failed — subset/parity checks both reported 'write' as an extra item. Contract restored from backup; git diff confirmed no residual change.
fix_b_python.green_after: Both tests pass after restoring the shared contract.

fix_b_ts.red_perturbation: Removed 'search' from the single shared src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json, then ran node --test on dist/tests/surface.test.js.
fix_b_ts.red_result: 2 failed — 'public surface is the governed allowlist' and 'surface parity: TS and Python read one shared allowlist'. This also demonstrates the anti-drift property: a one-place change to the shared file is seen by BOTH bindings (Python suite failed on the same perturbation). Contract restored; 'search' confirmed present again.

byte_freeze_verified.verified_on: both the worktree branch head and the merged local main (b86ef63)

additional_changes_made_in_scope.python_non_command_exclusion_set: _ENGINE_NON_COMMAND = _INSTRUMENTATION | {open_report, path, config} (drain, counters, set_profiling, set_slow_threshold_ms, attach_logging_subscriber, open_report, path, config)
additional_changes_made_in_scope.ts_non_command_exclusion_set: ENGINE_NON_COMMAND = INSTRUMENTATION + {openReport, config} (drain, counters, setProfiling, setSlowThresholdMs, attachSubscriber, openReport, config); the _native getter is _-prefixed and excluded by the dunder/private filter
additional_changes_made_in_scope.reverted_out_of_scope: src/ts/package-lock.json was touched by `npm install` (a stale 0.6.1 -> 0.7.2 version sync unrelated to this slice) and was reverted via git checkout before commit; not committed.
additional_changes_made_in_scope.prettier_formatting: prettier --write applied to surface.test.ts and the new JSON fixture to satisfy the formatting gate; content semantics unchanged.

agent_verify_result.ts_surface_suite: surface.test.ts + no-recovery-surface.test.ts: 14 pass / 0 fail; full `npm test` 61 pass / 0 fail
agent_verify_result.pyright_src_python: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 informations (native ext was built by the editable install, so no reportMissingModuleSource here)
agent_verify_result.ruff: clean (pre-commit ran ruff: All checks passed)
agent_verify_result.byte_freeze: three recovery suites + bindings.md zero-line diff vs baseline
agent_verify_result.scope: only src/python/tests/test_surface.py, src/ts/tests/surface.test.ts, and new src/conformance/governed-surface-allowlist.json changed; no spec/runtime/Rust change; canonical clean throughout (only pre-existing orchestrator review artifacts untracked under dev/plans/runs/)

next_step_for_orchestrator: Run the codex §9 re-review on local main b86ef63 to confirm both [P1]s are resolved: (1) Fix A introspects the real public surface in both bindings (Engine.delete would fail the subset check — demonstrated), (2) Fix B has a single shared allowlist both suites read (cross-binding drift fails a suite — demonstrated). Honest-green preserved (live surface = five core, read.* not asserted live). Byte-freeze held. No push/PR was performed. NOTE: src/ts/package-lock.json carries a pre-existing version-sync drift (lock 0.6.1 vs package.json 0.7.2) unrelated to this slice — flagged here, not touched.
